Test Title: Page renders heading 'Create your account'
Test Steps: 1. Navigate to /auth/signup
Expected Result: H1 text 'Create your account' is visible on page

Test Title: Subtitle 'Free forever · No credit card needed' is visible
Test Steps: 1. Navigate to /auth/signup; 2. Observe subtitle below heading
Expected Result: Subtitle renders: 'Free forever · No credit card needed'

Test Title: Full Name field renders with placeholder 'Jane Smith'
Test Steps: 1. Observe Full Name input
Expected Result: Placeholder text 'Jane Smith' visible; type='text'

Test Title: Email field renders with placeholder 'you@example.com'
Test Steps: 1. Observe Email input
Expected Result: Placeholder text 'you@example.com' visible; type='email'

Test Title: Password field renders with placeholder 'Min. 6 characters'
Test Steps: 1. Observe Password input
Expected Result: Placeholder text 'Min. 6 characters' visible; type='password'

Test Title: Password input masks characters as dots
Test Steps: 1. Click Password field; 2. Type characters
Expected Result: All typed characters appear as masked dots

Test Title: All fields are empty on initial load
Preconditions: Fresh page load; no prior form state
Test Steps: 1. Navigate to /auth/signup; 2. Observe all fields
Expected Result: Full Name, Email, Password fields are blank

Test Title: Submit with empty Full Name triggers required validation
Test Steps: 1. Leave Full Name blank; 2. Fill Email and Password; 3. Click 'Create Account'
Expected Result: Browser native required validation fires on Full Name; form does not submit

Test Title: Submit with empty Email triggers required validation
Test Steps: 1. Fill Full Name; 2. Leave Email blank; 3. Fill Password; 4. Click 'Create Account'
Expected Result: Browser native required validation fires on Email; form does not submit

Test Title: Submit with empty Password triggers required validation
Test Steps: 1. Fill Full Name and Email; 2. Leave Password blank; 3. Click 'Create Account'
Expected Result: Browser native required validation fires on Password; form does not submit

Test Title: Password exactly 5 chars shows 'Password must be at least 6 characters.' error
Test Steps: 1. Fill Name and Email; 2. Enter 5-char password; 3. Click 'Create Account'
Test Data: Name: Jane; Email: j@t.com; Password: abc12
Expected Result: Error banner appears: 'Password must be at least 6 characters.'; no API call made

Test Title: Password exactly 6 chars does not trigger frontend error
Test Steps: 1. Fill Name and Email; 2. Enter exactly 6-char password; 3. Click 'Create Account'
Test Data: Name: Jane; Email: edge@t.com; Password: abc123
Expected Result: No frontend password error; form proceeds to API call

Test Title: Password 1 char shows frontend error
Test Steps: 1. Fill Name and Email; 2. Enter 1-char password; 3. Click 'Create Account'
Expected Result: Error banner: 'Password must be at least 6 characters.' shown

Test Title: Invalid email format triggers HTML5 email validation
Test Steps: 1. Fill Name; 2. Enter 'notanemail' in Email; 3. Fill Password; 4. Click 'Create Account'
Expected Result: Browser native email validation fires; form does not submit

Test Title: Button shows 'Creating account...' during submission
Preconditions: Page loaded; API responding with delay
Test Steps: 1. Fill all valid fields; 2. Click 'Create Account'; 3. Observe button label immediately
Test Data: Name: Jane; Email: new@t.com; Password: pass123
Expected Result: Button label changes to 'Creating account...' while request is in flight

Test Title: Button is disabled during form submission
Preconditions: Page loaded; API responding with delay
Test Steps: 1. Fill all valid fields; 2. Click 'Create Account'; 3. Attempt second click during loading
Test Data: Name: Jane; Email: new@t.com; Password: pass123
Expected Result: Button has disabled attribute; second click has no effect; cursor is 'not-allowed'

Test Title: Button background changes to muted color when loading
Preconditions: Page loaded; API responding with delay
Test Steps: 1. Fill all valid fields; 2. Click 'Create Account'; 3. Observe button style
Test Data: Name: Jane; Email: new@t.com; Password: pass123
Expected Result: Button background switches from accent color to muted (var(--border2)); text color becomes var(--text3)

Test Title: Error banner renders with red/rose styling
Test Steps: 1. Enter 4-char password; 2. Click 'Create Account'; 3. Observe error container
Expected Result: Error banner visible with rose background and border; error text displayed in red

Test Title: Error banner clears on next submit attempt
Preconditions: A visible error banner from previous failed submit
Test Steps: 1. Trigger password-too-short error; 2. Fix password to 6+ chars; 3. Click 'Create Account'
Expected Result: Previous error banner disappears when new submission starts

Test Title: 'Sign in' link navigates to /auth/login
Test Steps: 1. Click 'Sign in' link below the form card
Expected Result: Browser navigates to /auth/login

Test Title: 'Continue as guest (3h session)' link navigates to /chat
Test Steps: 1. Click 'Continue as guest (3h session)' link at bottom of card
Expected Result: Browser navigates to /chat

Test Title: Navbar NexusAI logo navigates to /
Test Steps: 1. Click 'NexusAI' logo in top-left navbar
Expected Result: Browser navigates to /

Test Title: Navbar 'Sign In' button navigates to /auth/login
Test Steps: 1. Click 'Sign In' button in top-right navbar
Expected Result: Browser navigates to /auth/login

Test Title: Navbar 'Get Started' button links to /auth/signup
Test Steps: 1. Click 'Get Started' button in top-right navbar
Expected Result: Browser stays on /auth/signup (same route)

Test Title: Full Name field accepts special characters and numbers
Test Steps: 1. Enter name with symbols and numbers in Full Name field; 2. Observe value
Expected Result: Field accepts and displays full value without truncation or error

Test Title: Browser tab title is correct
Test Steps: 1. Navigate to /auth/signup; 2. Observe browser tab title
Expected Result: Tab title: 'NexusAI — AI Model Hub · Discover, Compare & Deploy'

Test Title: POST /api/auth/signup with valid data returns 201 with tokens and user
Preconditions: Backend running at localhost:3001; email not previously registered
Test Steps: 1. POST /api/auth/signup with valid body
Expected Result: HTTP 201; body contains accessToken, refreshToken, user: { _id, name, email }

Test Title: POST /api/auth/signup response user object excludes password field
Test Steps: 1. POST /api/auth/signup with valid body; 2. Inspect response user object
Expected Result: Response user has _id, name, email only; password field absent

Test Title: POST /api/auth/signup accessToken expires in 15 minutes
Test Steps: 1. POST /api/auth/signup; 2. Decode JWT accessToken
Expected Result: JWT exp = iat + 900 seconds

Test Title: POST /api/auth/signup refreshToken expires in 7 days
Test Steps: 1. POST /api/auth/signup; 2. Decode JWT refreshToken
Expected Result: JWT exp = iat + 604800 seconds

Test Title: POST /api/auth/signup with duplicate email returns 409 'Email already in use'
Test Steps: 1. POST /api/auth/signup with existing email
Expected Result: HTTP 409; message: 'Email already in use'

Test Title: POST /api/auth/signup with missing name returns 400
Test Steps: 1. POST /api/auth/signup without name field
Expected Result: HTTP 400; validation error on name field

Test Title: POST /api/auth/signup with missing email returns 400
Test Steps: 1. POST /api/auth/signup without email field
Expected Result: HTTP 400; validation error on email field

Test Title: POST /api/auth/signup with missing password returns 400
Test Steps: 1. POST /api/auth/signup without password field
Expected Result: HTTP 400; validation error on password field

Test Title: POST /api/auth/signup with invalid email format returns 400
Test Steps: 1. POST /api/auth/signup with non-email string
Expected Result: HTTP 400; @IsEmail validation error

Test Title: POST /api/auth/signup with password under 6 chars returns 400
Test Steps: 1. POST /api/auth/signup with 5-char password
Expected Result: HTTP 400; @MinLength(6) validation error on password

Test Title: POST /api/auth/signup with empty body returns 400
Test Steps: 1. POST /api/auth/signup with empty JSON body {}
Expected Result: HTTP 400; validation errors for all required fields

Test Title: POST /api/auth/signup with password exactly 6 chars returns 201
Preconditions: Backend running; email not previously registered
Test Steps: 1. POST /api/auth/signup with 6-char password
Expected Result: HTTP 201; signup succeeds

Test Title: GET /api/auth/signup returns 404 or 405
Test Steps: 1. Send GET /api/auth/signup
Expected Result: HTTP 404 or 405; endpoint only accepts POST

Test Title: POST /api/auth/signup with name as integer returns 400
Test Steps: 1. POST /api/auth/signup with numeric name
Expected Result: HTTP 400; @IsString validation error on name

Test Title: POST /api/auth/signup with very long name (500 chars) does not crash server
Test Steps: 1. POST /api/auth/signup with 500-char name string
Expected Result: HTTP 201 or 400; no HTTP 500 returned

Test Title: Successful signup redirects UI to /dashboard
Preconditions: Backend running; email not previously registered
Test Steps: 1. Fill valid Name/Email/Password; 2. Click 'Create Account'
Test Data: Name: Jane Smith; Email: new@nexus.com; Password: pass123
Expected Result: Browser navigates to /dashboard after 201 response

Test Title: Successful signup stores user/accessToken/refreshToken in auth store
Preconditions: Backend running; email not previously registered
Test Steps: 1. Fill valid form; 2. Submit; 3. Inspect auth store after redirect
Test Data: Name: Jane; Email: store@test.com; Password: pass123
Expected Result: useAuthStore contains user, accessToken, refreshToken from API response

Test Title: Duplicate email from API shows 'Email already in use' in error banner
Test Steps: 1. Enter used@test.com as email; 2. Fill Name and Password; 3. Submit
Expected Result: Error banner appears in UI with message 'Email already in use'

Test Title: API error without message field shows fallback 'Registration failed. Please try again.'
Preconditions: Backend returns error response body without message key
Test Steps: 1. Trigger server error with no message; 2. Observe UI error banner
Expected Result: Error banner shows 'Registration failed. Please try again.'

Test Title: Submit button re-enables after failed API call
Test Steps: 1. Submit form with duplicate email; 2. Observe button state after error
Expected Result: Button returns to enabled; label shows 'Create Account'; cursor is pointer

Test Title: Form is re-submittable after failed API response without page reload
Preconditions: Backend running; first submit failed with 409
Test Steps: 1. Submit with duplicate email; 2. Change email; 3. Click 'Create Account' again
Expected Result: Second submission fires a new API call without needing page refresh

Test Title: Frontend password validation prevents API call for password < 6 chars
Preconditions: Page loaded at /auth/signup; network monitoring active
Test Steps: 1. Fill Name and Email; 2. Enter 4-char password; 3. Click 'Create Account'; 4. Monitor network
Expected Result: No request sent to /api/auth/signup; error shown immediately from client side

Test Title: Form sends name/email/password JSON body to POST /api/auth/signup
Test Steps: 1. Fill all fields with valid data; 2. Click 'Create Account'; 3. Inspect outgoing request
Test Data: Name: Jane Smith; Email: j@test.com; Password: pass123
Expected Result: POST body contains exactly { name, email, password }; Content-Type: application/json

Test Title: Network error (backend unreachable) shows fallback error banner
Test Steps: 1. Stop backend; 2. Fill all valid form fields; 3. Click 'Create Account'
Test Data: Name: Jane; Email: j@test.com; Password: pass123
Expected Result: Error banner appears: 'Registration failed. Please try again.'
